comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
I'm not very familiar with the code base, but that change seems to work. Would that be the right strategy to implement #1778 @TomSweeneyRedHat ?
This change needs more assertion to prevent an invalid max-layers argument, and the new history order may be incorrect.
Not hugely familiar with this code, but should --squash set the --squash-max-layers flag to1?
Last commit renamed the argument to "--max-layers" as it feels more natural.
Last commit fixed the history order. Please let me know if the implementation is correct or how can it be improved.
Thanks for the review, I'll push a few more commits soon.

Turns out `store.Diff()` doesn't return the from layer content when from != to.Parent, thus this only work when committing an image built `from scratch`. When testing with `from image`, the resulting commit is missing the original from layer.

It seems like the storage driver operate differently when from != to.Parent and I'm not sure how to produce a diff that includes the from layer.

I couldn't find why `store.Diff()` doesn't work as expected. Actually `store.Mount()` work better and https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1790/commits/999a5cb2a76236304891cd790e424cc5fada7e5a refactor the logic a bit as mounting is easier to use: only the top layerID needs to be tracked.

With this last update, I was able to squash a 50 layers (6GB) fedora image down to 8 layers (4.5GB).
@TristanCacqueray I could not tell from your last comment, whether or not you believe this patch is ready for merge?

@rhatdan the PR is working as expected. Though it could use a squash, I left the `store.Mount` fix in a separate commit on top as @TomSweeneyRedHat only reviewed the `store.Diff` strategy.

I think we are also waiting for @nalind feedback. And I'd be happy to follow-up suggestions.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably cc80ccc) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@nalind, could you peruse this please and we'll merge if it LGTY?

Perhaps `--max-layers` is not a good name. There actually multiple strategies that could be considered:

* [squash from 1 to n-max] + n-max..n   (the current implementation, discard the oldest layer, useful for rolling update of an image)
* [1] + [squash from 2 to n-max-1] + n-max-1..n   (to keep the initial layer) 
* 1..n-max + [squash from n-max to n]    (to keep the oldest, discard the newest, useful for rebasing updates to a multi-layer images)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably f995696) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TristanCacqueray needs a rebase
@TomSweeneyRedHat alright, rebase was trivial

@nalind, @rhatdan , @vrothberg PTAL
Thanks @TristanCacqueray !
@nalind @rhatdan @vrothberg RE-PING!

Actually I think the second use-case mentioned in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1790#issuecomment-534579845 would be more useful. On a big image, the current implementation is quite slow when the squash happens. I think this review shouldn't touch the initial layer, and let the user manage it manually, for example by using `--squash` periodically.
@nalind @mtrmac @vrothberg @giuseppe This is an old PR but might be worth moving forward on.
Since this PR has not been touched in a year, I am going to close.  Reopen if more work is going to be done on it.
